topic.what: Multi-layer agentic AI framework for SOC automation integrating perception, anticipatory reasoning, and risk-aware action planning
topic.how: Combines Narrative Counterfactual Engine for LLM-based hypothesis generation, Structural Simulation Engine for graph-constrained feasibility checks, and Risk Scoring Evaluation Module for policy-aware response ranking in closed-loop sense-reason-act cycle
topic.why_matters: SOC analysts face 100k+ alerts/day with 2-4 hours manual triage; existing SIEM/SOAR lack autonomous reasoning, structural grounding, and risk-aware action selection; 3.5M cybersecurity workforce gap intensifies need for adaptive automation

characteristics.evidence: POC demonstration with LANL authentication data on local workstation
characteristics.context: LLM reasoning dominates at 480ms (95% of total); suitable for SOC timelines measured in minutes

characteristics.evidence: NCE-generated hypotheses with confidence scores for credential misuse, Kerberos abuse, benign misconfiguration
characteristics.context: Demonstrated on authentication anomaly scenario; confidence supports response prioritization

characteristics.what_it_measures: Risk-aware action ranking balancing containment vs business impact
characteristics.vs_baseline: Fixed playbooks treat all actions equally
characteristics.context: Weights α=0.7, β=0.3 tunable for organizational risk tolerance

characteristics.what_itasures: Hypothesis filtering through structural feasibility validation
characteristics.vs_baseline: LLM copilots lack structural grounding; hallucinate infeasible paths
characteristics.evidence: SSE rejected H3 (benign misconfiguration) due to missing structural support; verified H1/H2 on 50-node enterprise graph
characteristics.context: Graph-based validation filters LLM outputs against topology and privilege boundaries

characteristics.what_itasures: Reduction in manual alert correlation effort
characteristics.vs_baseline: Industry data: 2-4 hours manual triage per analyst
characteristics.evidence: POC consolidated authentication events into single enriched incident object
characteristics.context: Small-scale evaluation; production impact requires further validation

characteristics.what_it_measures: Attack pattern detection using MITRE ATT&CK framework
characteristics.evidence: NCE anchors hypotheses to ATT&CK tactics, techniques, and procedures; covers credential theft, lateral movement, privilege escalation
characteristics.context: Current scope excludes zero-day detection and malware analysis

good_for: Anticipatory threat reasoning with MITRE ATT&CK grounding
Risk-aware containment action selection balancing security and business continuity
Hybrid enterprise environments with IAM and network topology visibility
not_for: Fully autonomous deployment without human oversight
requires: Enterprise IAM and network topology data

novelty: Multi-layer agentic architecture integrating perception, reasoning, and action in closed-loop SOC workflow
Narrative Counterfactual Engine for LLM-based multi-hypothesis attack progression generation
Structural Simulation Engine for graph-constrained feasibility validation against enterprise topology and privilege graphs
Risk Scoring Evaluation Module with composite scoring balancing containment effectiveness and business impact
open_problems: Testing limited to 5000-event LANL sample; continuous production telemetry integration needed
Enterprise graph uses 50 nodes only; scalability to large production estates unvalidated
ATT&CK mappings are rule-based; zero-day and novel adversary tactics not captured
Response actions tested in dry-run mode only; closed-loop feedback from operational systems not demonstrated
Business impact scoring uses simplified heuristics; live operational metrics integration required
LLM latency (480ms) may need optimization for higher event volumes
Human-in-the-loop checkpoints and production SOAR connector development needed
Multi-cloud and hybrid IT/OT environment support not yet implemented
